name: eyebrow
text: 25-minute craft presentation
textPreview: 25-minute craft presentation

name: title
text: Reliable AI agents need more than task success
textPreview: Reliable AI agents need more than task success

name: subtitle
text: What τ-bench and the agent reliability framework change about how ML systems are built.
textPreview: What τ-bench and the agent reliability framework change about how ML systems are built.

name: title-thesis-head
text: Core thesis
textPreview: Core thesis

name: title-thesis-body
text: A capable model can still be an unreliable product. The engineering question is repeatable behavior under real operating conditions.
textPreview: A capable model can still be an unreliable product. The engineering question is repeatable behavior under real operating conditions.

name: source
text: Based on: τ-bench (2024), Towards a Science of AI Agent Reliability, and the attached article.
textPreview: Based on: τ-bench (2024), Towards a Science of AI Agent Reliability, and the attached article.

name: section
text: WHY IT MATTERS
textPreview: WHY IT MATTERS

name: title
text: The production failure mode is behavioral, not just factual
textPreview: The production failure mode is behavioral, not just factual

name: replit-head
text: Replit
textPreview: Replit

name: replit-body
text: A coding agent deleted a live production database during a code freeze.
textPreview: A coding agent deleted a live production database during a code freeze.

name: air-head
text: Air Canada
textPreview: Air Canada

name: air-body
text: A chatbot invented a bereavement-refund policy; a tribunal rejected the airline's defense.
textPreview: A chatbot invented a bereavement-refund policy; a tribunal rejected the airline's defense.

name: operator-head
text: Operator
textPreview: Operator

name: operator-body
text: An agent bought eggs for $31.43 without asking for required confirmation.
textPreview: An agent bought eggs for $31.43 without asking for required confirmation.

name: nyc-head
text: NYC chatbot
textPreview: NYC chatbot

name: nyc-body
text: Ten journalists received ten different wrong answers from the same business chatbot.
textPreview: Ten journalists received ten different wrong answers from the same business chatbot.

name: takeaway
text: The common pattern: the system did something plausible once, then failed under policy, repetition, confirmation, or consistency pressure.
textPreview: The common pattern: the system did something plausible once, then failed under policy, repetition, confirmation, or consistency pressure.

name: section
text: PROBLEM FRAMING
textPreview: PROBLEM FRAMING

name: title
text: A single green run is weak evidence
textPreview: A single green run is weak evidence

name: one-line
text: Agents are stochastic systems assembled from a model, prompt, memory, tools, policies, permissions, orchestration, and external state.
textPreview: Agents are stochastic systems assembled from a model, prompt, memory, tools, policies, permissions, orchestration, and external state.

name: classic-head
text: Classic benchmark
textPreview: Classic benchmark

name: classic-body
text: Can the agent complete the task once?
textPreview: Can the agent complete the task once?

name: deploy-head
text: Production question
textPreview: Production question

name: deploy-body
text: Will it behave correctly, consistently, robustly, predictably, and safely across repeated real interactions?
textPreview: Will it behave correctly, consistently, robustly, predictably, and safely across repeated real interactions?

name: section
text: Τ-BENCH
textPreview: Τ-BENCH

name: title
text: τ-bench turns agent reliability into a repeatable test
textPreview: τ-bench turns agent reliability into a repeatable test

name: env-head
text: Production-like environment
textPreview: Production-like environment

name: env-body
text: Database, API tools, natural-language policy, simulated users, and annotated goal states.
textPreview: Database, API tools, natural-language policy, simulated users, and annotated goal states.

name: domains-head
text: Two concrete domains
textPreview: Two concrete domains

name: domains-body
text:
τ-retail: 115 tasks, 15 tools.
τ-airline: 50 tasks, 13 tools with harder policy logic.
textPreview: τ-retail: 115 tasks, 15 tools. | τ-airline: 50 tasks, 13 tools with harder policy logic.

name: grading-head
text: Outcome-based grading
textPreview: Outcome-based grading

name: grading-body
text: A run succeeds only if the database state is correct and the user-facing message contains required information.
textPreview: A run succeeds only if the database state is correct and the user-facing message contains required information.

name: formula
text: r = r_action x r_output in {0,1}
textPreview: r = r_action x r_output in {0,1}

name: note
text: The path can vary, but the endpoint is deterministic and cheap to re-score. That makes repeated trials practical.
textPreview: The path can vary, but the endpoint is deterministic and cheap to re-score. That makes repeated trials practical.

name: section
text: THE KEY METRIC
textPreview: THE KEY METRIC

name: title
text: pass@k rewards search; pass^k measures service reliability
textPreview: pass@k rewards search; pass^k measures service reliability

name: passat-head
text: pass@k
textPreview: pass@k

name: passat-body
text: At least one of k attempts succeeds. Useful when a system can choose among candidates.
textPreview: At least one of k attempts succeeds. Useful when a system can choose among candidates.

name: passpow-head
text: pass^k
textPreview: pass^k

name: passpow-body
text: All k attempts succeed. Useful when each customer gets one live execution.
textPreview: All k attempts succeed. Useful when each customer gets one live execution.

name: example
text: A 90% single-run agent has pass^8 ~= 43%.
textPreview: A 90% single-run agent has pass^8 ~= 43%.

name: section
text: Τ-BENCH RESULTS
textPreview: Τ-BENCH RESULTS

name: title
text: τ-bench showed capable agents were not yet dependable
textPreview: τ-bench showed capable agents were not yet dependable

name: Retail pass^1-label
text: Retail pass^1
textPreview: Retail pass^1

name: Airline pass^1-label
text: Airline pass^1
textPreview: Airline pass^1

name: Retail pass^8-label
text: Retail pass^8
textPreview: Retail pass^8

name: failure-head
text: Failure classes map to fixes
textPreview: Failure classes map to fixes

name: failure-body
text:
~55% wrong argument / info -> validation
~25% wrong policy decision -> policy boundary tests
~19% partial completion -> completion checks
textPreview: ~55% wrong argument / info -> validation | ~25% wrong policy decision -> policy boundary tests | ~19% partial completion -> completion ch...

name: policy-head
text: Real policies matter
textPreview: Real policies matter

name: policy-body
text: Removing the policy dropped retail by 4.4 points, but airline by 22.4 points.
textPreview: Removing the policy dropped retail by 4.4 points, but airline by 22.4 points.

name: source
text: Historical 2024 τ-bench figures from attached article.
textPreview: Historical 2024 τ-bench figures from attached article.

name: section
text: RELIABILITY FRAMEWORK
textPreview: RELIABILITY FRAMEWORK

name: title
text: Capability improved seven times faster than reliability
textPreview: Capability improved seven times faster than reliability

name: meaning-head
text: What the number means
textPreview: What the number means

name: meaning-body
text: Over 18 months on GAIA, accuracy climbed at 0.21 per year. Aggregate reliability climbed at 0.03 per year.
textPreview: Over 18 months on GAIA, accuracy climbed at 0.21 per year. Aggregate reliability climbed at 0.03 per year.

name: implication-head
text: Product implication
textPreview: Product implication

name: implication-body
text: A model upgrade can raise task success while leaving consistency, robustness, risk ranking, or safety almost unchanged.
textPreview: A model upgrade can raise task success while leaving consistency, robustness, risk ranking, or safety almost unchanged.

name: section
text: RELIABILITY FRAMEWORK
textPreview: RELIABILITY FRAMEWORK

name: title
text: Reliability has four dimensions that should not be averaged away
textPreview: Reliability has four dimensions that should not be averaged away

name: cons-head
text: Consistency
textPreview: Consistency

name: cons-body
text: Same task, same conditions, same result and sufficiently stable path.
textPreview: Same task, same conditions, same result and sufficiently stable path.

name: rob-head
text: Robustness
textPreview: Robustness

name: rob-body
text: Performance survives wording changes, tool faults, and environment changes.
textPreview: Performance survives wording changes, tool faults, and environment changes.

name: pred-head
text: Predictability
textPreview: Predictability

name: pred-body
text: Confidence identifies which tasks are safe to automate and which need routing.
textPreview: Confidence identifies which tasks are safe to automate and which need routing.

name: safe-head
text: Safety
textPreview: Safety

name: safe-body
text: Violation frequency and harm severity stay visible, especially tail events.
textPreview: Violation frequency and harm severity stay visible, especially tail events.

name: bottom
text: For leaders, this creates a release conversation: which dimension matters most for this product, workflow, and permission boundary?
textPreview: For leaders, this creates a release conversation: which dimension matters most for this product, workflow, and permission boundary?

name: section
text: DIMENSION 1
textPreview: DIMENSION 1

name: title
text: Consistency separates stable automation from random failure
textPreview: Consistency separates stable automation from random failure

name: stable-head
text: Systematic 90%
textPreview: Systematic 90%

name: stable-body
text: Fails on the same identifiable 10% of tasks. Those cases can route to humans.
textPreview: Fails on the same identifiable 10% of tasks. Those cases can route to humans.

name: random-head
text: Random 90%
textPreview: Random 90%

name: random-body
text: Fails unpredictably across every task class. The next live request may break.
textPreview: Fails unpredictably across every task class. The next live request may break.

name: label
text: Same final state, different operational risk
textPreview: Same final state, different operational risk

name: path1
text: Run A: check inventory -> charge -> ship
textPreview: Run A: check inventory -> charge -> ship

name: path2
text: Run B: charge -> check inventory -> ship
textPreview: Run B: charge -> check inventory -> ship

name: cons-bottom
text: Outcome consistency is not enough. Transactional systems also need path consistency for recoverability and auditability.
textPreview: Outcome consistency is not enough. Transactional systems also need path consistency for recoverability and auditability.

name: section
text: DIMENSION 2
textPreview: DIMENSION 2

name: title
text: Robustness fails where users vary: ordinary wording
textPreview: Robustness fails where users vary: ordinary wording

name: phrasing-head
text: Why this matters
textPreview: Why this matters

name: phrasing-body
text: Users rarely match benchmark wording. An agent can recover from a 503 and still fail when the same intent is phrased naturally.
textPreview: Users rarely match benchmark wording. An agent can recover from a 503 and still fail when the same intent is phrased naturally.

name: rob-formula
text: R = min(Acc_perturbed / Acc_baseline, 1)
textPreview: R = min(Acc_perturbed / Acc_baseline, 1)

name: section
text: DIMENSION 3
textPreview: DIMENSION 3

name: title
text: Predictability only helps if confidence ranks risk
textPreview: Predictability only helps if confidence ranks risk

name: cal-head
text: Calibration
textPreview: Calibration

name: cal-body
text: When the agent says 80%, does it succeed about 80% of the time?
textPreview: When the agent says 80%, does it succeed about 80% of the time?

name: disc-head
text: Discrimination
textPreview: Discrimination

name: disc-body
text: Do higher confidence scores actually separate likely successes from likely failures?
textPreview: Do higher confidence scores actually separate likely successes from likely failures?

name: brier-head
text: Brier score
textPreview: Brier score

name: brier-body
text: How costly are confidence errors on each individual task?
textPreview: How costly are confidence errors on each individual task?

name: decision-text
text: Selective automation needs discrimination. Without it, confidence may look reasonable on average but cannot support release gates or human-routing thresholds.
textPreview: Selective automation needs discrimination. Without it, confidence may look reasonable on average but cannot support release gates or huma...

name: section
text: DIMENSION 4
textPreview: DIMENSION 4

name: title
text: Safety is a tail-risk problem, so keep it separate
textPreview: Safety is a tail-risk problem, so keep it separate

name: freq-head
text: Violation frequency
textPreview: Violation frequency

name: freq-body
text: How often did the agent skip a required check, take an unauthorized action, or produce a forbidden outcome?
textPreview: How often did the agent skip a required check, take an unauthorized action, or produce a forbidden outcome?

name: harm-head
text: Harm severity
textPreview: Harm severity

name: harm-body
text: When a violation happens, is it low-impact friction or a high-severity event like a wrong charge or irreversible action?
textPreview: When a violation happens, is it low-impact friction or a high-severity event like a wrong charge or irreversible action?

name: safety-formula
text: R_saf = 1 - Pr(violation) x E[severity | violation]
textPreview: R_saf = 1 - Pr(violation) x E[severity | violation]

name: tail
text: A 1% catastrophic failure should not disappear inside a good average score.
textPreview: A 1% catastrophic failure should not disappear inside a good average score.

name: aviation
text: This matches safety-critical engineering: tail events get separate thresholds.
textPreview: This matches safety-critical engineering: tail events get separate thresholds.

name: section
text: SYNTHESIS
textPreview: SYNTHESIS

name: title
text: The same incidents become diagnosable reliability failures
textPreview: The same incidents become diagnosable reliability failures

name: synthesis-bottom
text: The value of the framework is not taxonomy. It tells teams which test, guardrail, or release gate to add.
textPreview: The value of the framework is not taxonomy. It tells teams which test, guardrail, or release gate to add.

name: section
text: PRACTICAL USE
textPreview: PRACTICAL USE

name: title
text: Reliability changes the agent development lifecycle
textPreview: Reliability changes the agent development lifecycle

name: step-title-0
text: Define envelope
textPreview: Define envelope

name: step-body-0
text: Supported intents, tools, permissions, languages, and failure modes.
textPreview: Supported intents, tools, permissions, languages, and failure modes.

name: step-title-1
text: Build eval environment
textPreview: Build eval environment

name: step-body-1
text: Real APIs, policies, simulated users, and goal-state grading.
textPreview: Real APIs, policies, simulated users, and goal-state grading.

name: step-title-2
text: Run repeated tests
textPreview: Run repeated tests

name: step-body-2
text: Report pass^k and perturb wording, tools, and environment.
textPreview: Report pass^k and perturb wording, tools, and environment.

name: step-title-3
text: Profile dimensions
textPreview: Profile dimensions

name: step-body-3
text: Report consistency, robustness, predictability, and safety separately.
textPreview: Report consistency, robustness, predictability, and safety separately.

name: step-title-4
text: Gate releases
textPreview: Gate releases

name: step-body-4
text: Set thresholds before pilot and production promotion.
textPreview: Set thresholds before pilot and production promotion.

name: step-title-5
text: Regress incidents
textPreview: Regress incidents

name: step-body-5
text: Turn production failures into durable evaluation cases.
textPreview: Turn production failures into durable evaluation cases.

name: section
text: PRACTICAL USE
textPreview: PRACTICAL USE

name: title
text: The leadership decision is where autonomy is earned
textPreview: The leadership decision is where autonomy is earned

name: evals-head
text: Evaluation
textPreview: Evaluation

name: evals-body
text: Repeated and perturbed tests decide where the agent is trustworthy.
textPreview: Repeated and perturbed tests decide where the agent is trustworthy.

name: gates-head
text: Release gates
textPreview: Release gates

name: gates-body
text: Thresholds decide when to move from experiment to pilot to production.
textPreview: Thresholds decide when to move from experiment to pilot to production.

name: contain-head
text: Containment
textPreview: Containment

name: contain-body
text: Permissions, confirmations, and rollback limit damage when evaluation is wrong.
textPreview: Permissions, confirmations, and rollback limit damage when evaluation is wrong.

name: leader
text: For product and engineering leaders, reliability is not a model-selection afterthought. It is an operating contract for which workflows the agent may own.
textPreview: For product and engineering leaders, reliability is not a model-selection afterthought. It is an operating contract for which workflows t...

name: section
text: CLOSE
textPreview: CLOSE

name: title
text: Measure behavior, limit damage, then scale autonomy
textPreview: Measure behavior, limit damage, then scale autonomy

name: bullets
text:
• Task success answers whether an agent can complete a task once.
• Reliability answers whether it behaves correctly across repeated, varied, real interactions.
• τ-bench supplies repeatable environments and pass^k.
• The reliability framework separates consistency, robustness, predictability, and safety.
• The engineering discipline is ADLC: evals, gates, monitoring, containment, and regression tests.
textPreview: • Task success answers whether an agent can complete a task once. | • Reliability answers whether it behaves correctly across repeated, v...

name: final
text: The Replit agent could write code but could not be trusted. Closing that gap requires reliability engineering, not just another model release.
textPreview: The Replit agent could write code but could not be trusted. Closing that gap requires reliability engineering, not just another model rel...
